description: Canonical catalog of Banking & Capital Markets institution types used by the Client Role-Aware Regulatory Interpretation engine. Restricted to B&CM only - Insurance, Payments-only, Non-bank Consumer Finance and FinTech/RegTech are intentionally out of scope. Every entry drives the multi-select dropdown on Page 1 and the deterministic applicability scoring for the selected regulation. Update this file (or ship an overriding file via the REGAI_INSTITUTION_TYPES_PATH environment variable) to add new institution types WITHOUT touching Python code.

institution_types.name: Global Systemically Important Bank (G-SIB)
institution_types.summary: Cross-border banking group designated by the FSB as systemically important; subject to the highest capital, resolution and disclosure requirements globally.

institution_types.name: Domestic Systemically Important Bank (D-SIB)
institution_types.summary: Bank designated by its home supervisor as systemically important to the domestic market; subject to elevated buffers and supervisory scrutiny.

institution_types.name: Universal Bank
institution_types.summary: Financial group offering both commercial and investment banking services under one licence.

institution_types.name: Commercial Bank
institution_types.summary: Deposit-taking institution offering lending, payments, cash management and treasury services to corporate and retail clients.

institution_types.name: Retail Bank
institution_types.summary: Consumer-focused bank offering accounts, mortgages, cards and personal lending.

institution_types.name: Corporate Bank
institution_types.summary: Provides commercial lending, cash management, trade finance and treasury services to corporate clients.

institution_types.name: Wholesale Bank
institution_types.summary: Bank whose clients are exclusively financial institutions, corporates and public bodies; no retail deposit-taking.

institution_types.name: Private Bank
institution_types.summary: High-net-worth wealth management with discretionary and advisory investment services.

institution_types.name: Investment Bank
institution_types.summary: Deals in securities, underwriting, M&A advisory, trading and capital markets services for institutional clients.

institution_types.name: Digital Bank
institution_types.summary: Digital-only bank offering payments, deposits and lending primarily through mobile and web channels.

institution_types.name: Challenger Bank
institution_types.summary: New-entrant bank competing with incumbents through digital channels and simpler product ranges.

institution_types.name: Neo Bank
institution_types.summary: Digital-first bank often operating on a partner licence; typically thin-balance-sheet with product distribution focus.

institution_types.name: Small Finance Bank
institution_types.summary: Specialised bank providing basic banking services to under-served or micro / small business segments.

institution_types.name: Cooperative Bank
institution_types.summary: Member-owned bank that mutualises retail / SME banking services.

institution_types.name: Regional Bank
institution_types.summary: Bank whose franchise is concentrated in a specific geographic region; typically mid-sized with retail and SME lending.

institution_types.name: Community Bank
institution_types.summary: Small locally-focused bank serving a defined community; deposit-led with relationship-based lending.

institution_types.name: Savings Bank
institution_types.summary: Institution focused on deposit-taking and low-risk lending, often with a regional or community mandate.

institution_types.name: Foreign Bank Branch
institution_types.summary: Branch of a foreign parent bank operating locally under host-country supervision; carries the parent's licence.

institution_types.name: Islamic Bank
institution_types.summary: Bank operating under Shariah principles; profit-sharing and asset-backed financing instead of interest-based lending.

institution_types.name: Development Bank
institution_types.summary: State-owned or multilateral bank providing long-term finance for infrastructure and development objectives.

institution_types.name: Central Bank
institution_types.summary: State-owned monetary authority: monetary policy, payment systems, financial-stability oversight and banknote issuance.

institution_types.name: Custodian Bank
institution_types.summary: Safekeeps assets on behalf of institutional clients; provides settlement, corporate actions and fund services.

institution_types.name: Correspondent Bank
institution_types.summary: Bank providing services (payments, FX, trade finance) to another bank, usually across borders.

institution_types.name: Broker Dealer (Small)
institution_types.summary: Small broker-dealer executing client orders in securities with limited proprietary trading and simpler operating model.

institution_types.name: Broker Dealer (Mid-sized)
institution_types.summary: Mid-sized broker-dealer with material trading activity and multiple product lines / venues.

institution_types.name: Broker Dealer (Large)
institution_types.summary: Large broker-dealer with cross-asset trading, market-making, prime brokerage and systemic footprint.

institution_types.name: Investment Firm
institution_types.summary: MiFID-authorised firm providing investment services (advice, execution, portfolio management) to professional or retail clients.

institution_types.name: Securities Firm
institution_types.summary: Regulated firm dealing in securities on behalf of clients or as principal; may hold trading, dealing or arranging permissions.

institution_types.name: Asset Management Company
institution_types.summary: Firm organised to manage collective investment vehicles (UCITS / AIFs) as its principal business.

institution_types.name: Asset Manager
institution_types.summary: Manages collective / segregated investment portfolios; UCITS and/or AIFMD authorised.

institution_types.name: Wealth Manager
institution_types.summary: Discretionary / advisory investment management for private clients.

institution_types.name: Portfolio Manager
institution_types.summary: Firm or business unit managing discretionary or advisory investment mandates for institutional or private clients.

institution_types.name: Fund Manager
institution_types.summary: Firm managing one or more investment funds; may sit under a UCITS ManCo or an AIFM depending on the vehicle.

institution_types.name: Hedge Fund Manager
institution_types.summary: AIFM managing alternative investment funds using leverage, derivatives and short-selling to pursue absolute returns.

institution_types.name: Mutual Fund / UCITS Manager
institution_types.summary: UCITS-style retail fund manager with daily liquidity, eligible-assets rules and diversification constraints.

institution_types.name: Alternative Investment Fund Manager (AIFM)
institution_types.summary: Firm authorised under AIFMD to manage one or more alternative investment funds (hedge, PE, real estate, infrastructure).

institution_types.name: Fund Administrator (Small)
institution_types.summary: Small fund administrator delivering NAV, transfer agency and regulatory reporting to a handful of fund clients.

institution_types.name: Fund Administrator (Mid-sized)
institution_types.summary: Mid-sized fund administrator servicing multiple fund ranges and asset classes.

institution_types.name: Fund Administrator (Large)
institution_types.summary: Large multi-jurisdiction fund administrator servicing thousands of funds across UCITS, AIF, PE, real estate and infrastructure.

institution_types.name: Prime Broker
institution_types.summary: Bank / broker providing bundled services (financing, custody, securities lending, execution) to hedge funds and institutional clients.

institution_types.name: Custodian
institution_types.summary: Firm safekeeping securities and cash for institutional clients; may be a specialised (non-bank) custodian or a securities services division.

institution_types.name: Depository Participant
institution_types.summary: Intermediary providing investors with access to a central securities depository (CSD); handles demat holdings and corporate actions.

institution_types.name: Central Securities Depository (CSD)
institution_types.summary: Provides securities settlement and safekeeping of securities in book-entry form.

institution_types.name: Central Counterparty (CCP)
institution_types.summary: Central counterparty interposing itself between buyers and sellers to clear derivatives / securities trades; systemically important.

institution_types.name: Clearing House
institution_types.summary: Multilateral clearer for derivatives / securities trades; typically operates one or more CCPs and settlement services.

institution_types.name: Exchange / Stock Exchange
institution_types.summary: Regulated market operator hosting trading of shares, bonds, derivatives and other listed instruments.

institution_types.name: Trading Venue (MTF/OTF)
institution_types.summary: Multilateral or organised trading facility bringing together buying and selling interests in financial instruments under MiFID rules.

institution_types.name: Market Operator
institution_types.summary: Entity that operates a regulated market, MTF, OTF, exchange or benchmark administration platform.

institution_types.name: Securities Lending Agent
institution_types.summary: Agent lending securities on behalf of beneficial owners; manages collateral and counterparty risk.

institution_types.name: Transfer Agent
institution_types.summary: Firm maintaining the register of holders of a fund or issuer; handles subscriptions, redemptions and record-keeping.

institution_types.name: Registrar
institution_types.summary: Firm maintaining the register of shareholders / bondholders on behalf of an issuer; handles corporate actions and dividend payments.

institution_types.name: Trustee Services
institution_types.summary: Trustee acting for bond issuances, unit trusts, structured products or fund vehicles; holds assets and enforces documentation.

institution_types.name: Capital Markets Infrastructure Provider
institution_types.summary: Post-trade / pre-trade infrastructure vendor providing critical services to trading venues, clearers and depositaries.
